{"step_by_step_thinking": "The documents discuss the effectiveness of sealants in preventing and arresting noncavitated carious lesions. Specifically, Document [12] states 'Sealants can be applied to occlusal surfaces, as well as to smooth and proximal surfaces. Research supports their use in preventing both occlusal surface caries and arresting noncavitated and microcavitated lesions on proximal surfaces.' This indicates that sealants are effective in preventing and arresting initial carious lesions, which aligns with option A. Options B, C, and D refer to more advanced stages of caries that are not mentioned as being effectively managed by sealants.", "answer_choice": "A"}
